title: 2-Year Treasury Yield (DGS2)
summary: The 2-Year Treasury Yield stood at 3.78% as of April 9, 2026, down 1 basis point from the prior day (3.79%) and down 6 basis points from the start of the week (3.84% on April 6). The yield has exhibited a modest declining trend over the observed period, moving from 3.84% on April 3 to 3.78% on April 9.
key_points: Prior reading: 3.79% (April 8, 2026), change of -0.01 percentage point
Absolute decline over observation period: -0.06 percentage points
Direction: Downward trend across all consecutive observations
why_it_matters: The 2-year Treasury yield is a key market-based indicator of near-term interest rate expectations and policy outlook. Movements in this yield reflect shifts in market pricing of Fed rate expectations over the next 2 years. A declining 2-year yield may signal dovish repricing or easing financial conditions.
macro_context: The 2-year yield at 3.78% implies market expectations for the federal funds rate in the near term. This data point represents a market-derived expectation rather than an explicit forecast or policy decision. Interpretation requires additional context on Fed policy expectations.

issues_found: event_type 'data_release_other' is questionable for continuous market yield data; this is not a periodic data release like CPI or employment reports but rather a daily market observation
Source is a raw data feed rather than an analytical document or article; contains no commentary, analysis, or catalyst interpretation
The record's 'why_it_matters' and macro_context sections are general knowledge about 2-year yields, not derived from the source itself
market_impact directional_bias set to 'unknown' appropriately reflects lack of interpretive content in source

notes: This source is a FRED data snapshot providing raw 2-year Treasury yield observations. It is not an analytical document, news article, or policy commentary. The data is relevant to macro catalysts as the 2-year yield reflects near-term rate expectations, but the source itself contains no analysis or catalyst interpretation. Confidence is moderate (5/10) given the factual accuracy of the data points, but the source provides no narrative context for why yields are moving or what they imply for policy.
